rationale: My round-1 ask is fully ADDRESSED via the alternative path I offered (narrowly specify R6 + prove prose mentions are ignored). (1) Extraction rule pinned to anchored link bullets only: R6 is retitled 'anchored links only' and defines a reference as 'only an anchored markdown-link touchpoint bullet — a bullet of the shape `- [ADR-XXXX](<path>): …`', with bare `ADR-####` prose mentions inside a bullet's text or a section paragraph explicitly not extracted and not resolved. (2) Self-note present: R6 calls out that 110's own ADR-0037 bullet mentions ADR-0040 and ADR-0030 in prose, correctly outside its own extraction, so 110 satisfies its own rule — verified against line 36, which does carry both mentions in prose, no separate anchored bullet. (3) AC boundary triple (TestValidateSpec_ADRTouchpointExtractionBoundary) covers both directions: (a) anchored link to nonexistent ADR-9999 fails, (b) bare-prose `per ADR-9999` does not fail, (c) 110-shaped spec passes with no adr-coverage-* leak. R6's Falsified clause pins both directions symmetrically. The prose-not-cited resolution the 109→110→111 series depends on is now internally consistent and testable. No residual blocker.

findings.issue: Round-1 REQUEST_CHANGES resolved: R6 extraction rule now scoped to anchored markdown-link touchpoint bullets; bare-prose ADR mentions (incl. 110's own ADR-0040/ADR-0030) explicitly excluded; self-inconsistency note added; AC line 101 proves prose is ignored while anchored links resolve.
findings.impact: The spec no longer models a failing case against itself; the touchpoint-extraction contract is now stated tightly enough that a second interpretation is falsifiable by test.
